ID:       ddx-70a432b5
Title:    Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit event
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   ac-quality:needs-refinement, phase:2, story:16
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-a8a18cb7
Deps:     ddx-f04faa86, ddx-4cd64068, ddx-90e6d29d, ddx-18d83a6a, ddx-b4c5151a, ddx-17388615, ddx-1664e1d2, ddx-f7c9159f, ddx-544ab414, ddx-a26cafca, ddx-84d6da52, ddx-371ec7f8, ddx-0285599d, ddx-7043796a
Desc:     PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.
Accept:   1. FEAT-008 spec updated with Run-detail-page subsection + trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist.

The run-detail page and evidence browser need a single end-to-end regression test that exercises prompt, response, tool trace, and evidence together, plus the p95 dataset size check. The current Playwright coverage only checks the evidence tab slice.

120	
121	**URL deprecation policy.** During the deprecation window, the legacy
122	URLs respond with **302 redirects (NOT 301)** to the matching filtered
123	Runs URL with all query params preserved, plus a `Sunset` response
124	header that names the cutoff date for the redirects:
125	
126	| Legacy URL | Redirect target |
127	|---|---|
128	| `/sessions` | `/runs?layer=run` |
129	| `/sessions#<id>` | `/runs?layer=run&session=<id>` |
130	| `/executions[?bead=&verdict=&harness=&q=&after=]` | `/runs?layer=try` with the original query string preserved verbatim |
131	| `/executions/[id]` | `/runs/[runId]?bundle=true` |
132	
133	302 (not 301) is intentional: bookmarks and external link aggregators
134	should not cache the redirect permanently while we still own the legacy
135	routes. The redirect response carries `Sunset: <RFC 9745 date>` and the
136	project nav drops the Sessions/Executions entries once the redirects and
137	the internal-link sweep land (`shellRoutes.ts`, `BeadDetail.svelte`,

1375	| `actor` | Viewer identity derived from the authenticated project member |
1376	| `source` | `graphql:run` |
1377	| `body` | Single line: `project_id=<projectId> run_id=<runId> layer=<layer> visibility=project_membership` |
1378	
1379	### Run resolver contract
1380	
1381	The canonical deep loader for a run detail surface is `run(id:)`. It returns
1382	the run-layer fields used by FEAT-008:
1383	
1384	- `prompt`
1385	- `response`
1386	- `stderr`
1387	- `bundleFiles[]`
1388	
1389	The tool-call resolver is the `toolCalls(first, after)` stream exposed via
1390	`runToolCalls(id:, first:, after:)`. It returns the normalized tool-call
1391	stream as a paginated `RunToolCallConnection`, where each node projects the
1392	canonical drain-time `ToolCallEntry` shape:
1393	
1394	- `tool`
1395	- `input`
1396	- `output`
1397	- `duration_ms`
1398	- `error`
1399	
1400	`ToolCallEntry` is the normalized agent-side record of one tool execution. DDx
1401	persists the stream at drain time so the run substrate can page the stored
1402	sequence without rehydrating raw agent logs.
1403	
1404	The `bundleFile(path)` lookup is exposed via `runBundleFile(id:, path:)`. It
1405	is confined to the run's bundle root, rejects path traversal, absolute paths,
1406	and symlink escapes, and inlines content only for the whitelist of small text
1407	files documented by FEAT-008.
1408	
1409	### Layer-to-substrate mapping for the Runs UI
1410	
1411	The web Runs view (FEAT-008 §5, FEAT-021) renders three layer chips
1412	backed by the unified substrate plus the legacy detail backings. The
1413	shared run-detail tab strip is the canonical drill-down surface used by
1414	FEAT-008, FEAT-019, and FEAT-021:
1415	
1416	| Chip | Substrate row | Detail backing for row expansion |
1417	|---|---|---|
1418	| `work` | `layer: 3` Run record | Layer-3 record's queue inputs / stop-condition log / child layer-2 attempt ids; the shared tab strip exposes `overview` only |
1419	| `try` | `layer: 2` Run record | The `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle attached to the layer-2 record (manifest, prompt, result, checks, verdict); the shared tab strip exposes `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `tools`, and `evidence` |
1420	| `run` | `layer: 1` Run record | The associated `AgentSession` row (prompt / response / stderr / billing / cached-token detail) joined onto the layer-1 record; the shared tab strip exposes `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `session`, `tools`, and `evidence` |
1421	
1422	`AgentSession` rows that have no parent layer-2 attempt (raw `ddx
1423	agent log` invocations) surface as synthesized `layer=run` Runs rows
1424	keyed by session id, so no agent-session row is dropped during the

data.text: All spec updates are confirmed. Let me verify the e2e tests and then check if `bun run test:e2e` and `lefthook run pre-commit` pass.

All deliverables from the child beads are confirmed:
- FEAT-008: Run-detail page section with trust boundary + evidence whitelist (lines 101-113)
- FEAT-010: Full resolver contract with `prompt`, `response`, `stderr`, `bundleFiles[]`, `toolCalls(first, after)`, `bundleFile(path)`, `ToolCallEntry` definition + drain-time persistence note (lines 1379-1407)
- FEAT-019: `/runs/[runId]` deep page link (lines 104-108)
- `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` passes
- p95 test + full-stack e2e test both exist

This bead was decomposed into children (ddx-17388615, ddx-1664e1d2, ddx-f7c9159f) which are all closed. Writing `no_changes_rationale.txt` with a verification command that validates all ACs.

data.text:
Verification command passes. All ACs for `ddx-70a432b5` are satisfied by the closed child beads:

- **AC 1–3**: Spec files updated (FEAT-008 trust boundary + whitelist, FEAT-010 resolver contract + ToolCallEntry, FEAT-019 `/runs/[runId]` link)
- **AC 4**: `raw_transcript_viewed` audit event implemented in `resolver_runs.go` + `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` passes
- **AC 5**: Full-stack e2e test `full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence` in `runs.spec.ts`
- **AC 6**: p95 dataset test (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) in `runs-evidence.spec.ts`

No code changes needed in this execution — all work was completed by decomposed children ddx-17388615, ddx-1664e1d2, and ddx-f7c9159f.
